label: Beef
catNotes: Excellent for cats. Beef heart is one of the richest natural taurine sources.

label: Chicken
catNotes: Cats: prefer dark meat over breast (more taurine). Hearts highly recommended.

label: Turkey
catNotes: Cats: turkey heart is rich in taurine. Avoid skin if cat is overweight.

label: Lamb
notes: Novel protein; good for beef-sensitive dogs
catNotes: Fine for cats — slightly higher fat than beef. Lamb heart includes taurine.

label: Pork
catNotes: Cats: cook fully; otherwise same risks. Pork hearts an option for taurine.

label: Duck
catNotes: Excellent for cats. Duck heart is a good taurine source.

label: Rabbit
catNotes: Ideal cat protein — closest to wild prey. May need added fat (very lean).

label: Venison
catNotes: Good novel protein for cats; add fat source.

label: Salmon
notes: Raw only if frozen ≥7 days first. Watch Neorickettsia (Pacific NW).
catNotes: Cats: never raw freshwater fish (thiaminase). Cooked salmon is safe and a good omega-3 source. Pair with vitamin E.

label: Mackerel
catNotes: Cats: limit to ≤ 10% of intake — mercury + PUFA. Always pair with vitamin E.

label: Sardines
catNotes: Best fish for cats — low mercury, high omega-3. Limit to ½ sardine 2–3× weekly for a 4 kg cat.

label: Whitefish (cod, haddock)
catNotes: Cats: cook to inactivate thiaminase. Lean — pair with fat source.
